items.title: JSX expressions and attributes: reject the invalid model
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this React: Component-Based Browser Applications misconception? JSX strings inserted through braces are interpreted as raw HTML.
hints: Compare both options with platform semantics and observable user or browser evidence.

feedback.correct: Correct. React escapes ordinary JSX text expressions; raw HTML requires an explicit dangerous API and trusted sanitization.
feedback.incorrect: That preserves the misconception. React escapes ordinary JSX text expressions; raw HTML requires an explicit dangerous API and trusted sanitization.

criteria.description: Selects the source-linked React: Component-Based Browser Applications correction rather than the documented misconception.

items.prompt: Embed expressions, use React DOM property names and keep data distinct from executable markup. Build a bounded artifact demonstrating JSX expressions and React DOM attributes; the locked browser verifier checks observable structure, behavior, or computed presentation.
